topic: A technician performing a 'Three-Point Bend' test on a carbon fiber sample.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: front view of technician positioning the carbon fiber sample on the three-point bend rig.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: side-angle as the center indenter lowers, applying load to the sample.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: close-up of deflection reading as the technician records results.
parsed_generation.narration: A technician positions a carbon fiber sample on the three-point bend rig and secures it. The center indenter applies load, and a deflection readout is captured in the final shot.

feedback: Overall alignment with the topic is solid and the sequence uses clear camera angles and timestamps. It could better mirror the gold standard by emphasizing the bending progression toward fracture and by enriching the rig and material visuals. Adding explicit depiction of the two supports, center indenter specifics, and a measurement readout with slight narration context would improve alignment.
strengths: Clear, concise shot sequence with distinct camera angles and timestamps.
Narration closely tracks the action, reinforcing the three-point bend process.
fixes: Add carbon fiber texture, lighting cues, and rig details to boost visual richness.
Describe load progression toward fracture and ensure explicit depiction of supports, indenter, and deflection measurement in the shots.
